title: The Carbon Atom's Journey
description: Follow a single carbon atom through Earth's interconnected cycle — from air to life to rock and back again.

stages.title: A Carbon Atom's Journey
stages.body:
This carbon atom has been on Earth for **4.5 billion years**. It was forged in a dying star. It cycled through ancient seas, was buried and became limestone, rode a tectonic plate into a volcano, and erupted into the sky as CO₂.

About 200 years ago, humans started digging up carbon that had been buried for millions of years and burning it. **Every second, we release ~634 tons of CO₂** that nature took eons to store.

Where will *this* atom go next?

stages.title: Choose Your Path
stages.description: A carbon atom in the atmosphere can take many paths. Each one leads to a different fate — and a different timescale. Where does yours go?

paths.label: 🌿 Absorbed by a tree
paths.description: Photosynthesis pulls CO₂ from the air. The atom becomes part of a glucose molecule in a leaf in Brazil.

paths.label: 🌊 Dissolved in the ocean
paths.description: CO₂ dissolves into surface water, forming carbonic acid. The atom joins the ocean's vast carbon reservoir.

paths.label: 🔥 Burned in a fire
paths.description: A wildfire releases the atom back into the atmosphere. In California's 2020 fires, this happened to billions of tons.

paths.label: 🪨 Buried as limestone
paths.description: The atom is locked into calcium carbonate shells of marine organisms. Over millions of years, these become limestone rock.

paths.label: ⛽ Extracted and burned
paths.description: Humans extract ancient fossil carbon and burn it for energy. The atom joins the ~37.8 Gt CO₂ we emit each year.

stages.title: The Deep Time Reveal
stages.body:
You chose: **{{selectedPathData.label}}**

This path takes approximately **{{selectedPathData.timescale}}**. Compare that to:

- 🔥 Burning in fire: **minutes**
- 🌿 Tree absorption: **seconds to years**
- 🌊 Ocean mixing: **centuries to millennia**
- 🪨 Fossilization: **millions of years**

The problem? Humans are releasing carbon that took **300 million years** to bury — in just decades. We're moving carbon 10 million times faster than nature ever did.

*"Carbon is patient. We are not."*

questions.question: How many GtC do humans emit per year?
questions.explanation: In 2023, fossil fuel emissions were ~10.4 GtC (37.8 Gt CO₂). The other options are too low or too high.

questions.question: Which process removes CO₂ from the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis absorbs CO₂ and converts it to glucose + oxygen.

questions.question: How long does it take nature to bury carbon as fossil fuels?
questions.explanation: Fossil fuels formed over 300+ million years. Humans release them in decades.

questions.question: What's the current atmospheric CO₂ concentration (April 2026)?
questions.hint: It's above 420 ppm and rising ~2.5 ppm/year

stages.title: You Just Completed Module 1!
stages.body:
You now understand that carbon atoms cycle through Earth's systems on timescales from seconds to millions of years — and that humans are disrupting this cycle at unprecedented speed.

**Your score: {{score}} points** Now let's see the evidence of this disruption in the atmosphere itself.

callouts.label: Keeling Curve
callouts.value: The definitive record of atmospheric CO₂ — 1958 to today
